channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Egyptian officials say 17 people are missing after a tourist yacht sank in high waves on Red Sea
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — At least 17 people are missing after a tourist yacht sank in the Red Sea following warnings about rough seas, Egyptian officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Vatican to install a webcam at St. Peter's tomb as part technological outreach for Holy Year
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Roman Catholic faithful will be able to view St. Peter’s tomb remotely as part of the Vatican’s technological outreach with its far-flung flock during the upcoming Holy Year. Pope Francis will inaugurate a webcam on Dec. 2 to be installed beneath the main altar of St.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Egyptian officials say 17 people are missing after a tourist yacht sank in high waves on Red Sea
item.description: Egyptian officials say at least 17 people are missing after a tourist yacht sank in the Red Sea following warnings about rough seas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hundreds of homeowners in England and Wales battle floodwaters after weekend storm
item.description: Hundreds of homeowners in England and Wales were battling floodwaters after the second major storm of the winter brought widespread disruption to the U.K.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia reportedly captures a Briton fighting for Ukraine as Russian troops advance
item.description: Russia's military captured a British national fighting with Ukrainian troops who have occupied part of Russia's Kursk region, according to reports Monday, as Moscow began daylight drone attacks on civilian areas of Ukraine and its ground forces accelerated gains along parts of the front line.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French mass rape trial prosecutors demand maximum sentence for Gisèle Pelicot's ex-husband
item.description: A high-profile rape trial in France is moving into a new phase with prosecutors setting out the verdicts and punishments they want for 51 men men accused of raping Gisèle Pelicot, 71, while she was drugged and rendered unconscious by her husband of nearly 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The UAE arrests 3 Uzbek nationals for the killing of an Israeli-Moldovan rabbi
item.description: The United Arab Emirates says police have arrested three Uzbek nationals for the killing of an Israeli-Moldovan rabbi.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Philippine showdown: President says he'll fight vice president's plot to have him killed
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on Monday described a public threat by the vice president to have him killed by an assassin as a criminal plot and vowed to fight it, in a looming showdown between the country’s two top leaders.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Uruguay’s leftist opposition candidate Yamandú Orsi becomes country's new president
item.description: MONTEVIDEO, Uruguay (AP) — Uruguay's leftist opposition candidate, Yamandú Orsi, became the country's new president in a tight runoff Sunday, ousting the conservative governing coalition and making the South American nation the latest to rebuke the incumbent party in a year of landmark elections wor
item.dc:creator: NAYARA BATSCHKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea holds memorial for forced laborers at Sado mines, a day after boycotting Japanese event
item.description: SADO, Japan (AP) — South Korea paid tribute to wartime Korean forced laborers at Japan’s Sado Island Gold Mines in a memorial ceremony on Monday, a day after boycotting a similar event organized by Japan, as tensions over historical atrocities continue to strain relations between the two sides.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Landslide and flash floods hit Indonesia’s Sumatra island, leaving 16 dead and 6 missing
item.description: KARO, Indonesia (AP) — Rescuers in Indonesia recovered 16 bodies under tons of mud and rocks or that were swept away in flash floods that hit mountainside villages on Sumatra Island, officials said Monday. Six people are still missing, officials said.
item.dc:creator: BINSAR BAKKARA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DHL cargo plane crashes and skids into a house in Lithuania, killing Spanish crew member
item.description: A DHL cargo plane has crashed on approach to an airport in Lithuania's capital and skidded into a nearby house, killing a Spanish crew member.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lebanon's Shiite Muslims pay high price in war between Israel and Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Lebanese civilians most devastated by the Israel- Hezbollah war are Shiite Muslims, and many of them believe they are being unfairly punished because they share a religious identity with Hezbollah militants and often live in the same areas.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Namibia may elect its first-ever female president in elections this week
item.description: OSHAKATI, Namibia (AP) — Namibia's Vice President Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah could become the country’s first female president if she wins the presidential election Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ELIASER NDEYANALE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel moves toward ceasefire deal with Hezbollah: reports
item.description: Israel is reportedly moving toward a ceasefire agreement with Hezbollah in Lebanon after nearly a year of fighting escalated into an all-out war
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah fires barrage of rockets into Israel after IDF targets Hezbollah command centers in Beirut
item.description: Hezbollah on Sunday fired missiles and other projectiles into Israel in retaliation for to deadly IDF strikes on the militant group’s command centers in Beirut.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing Israeli rabbi who lived in UAE found dead, 3 arrested
item.description: A missing Israeli rabbi was found dead with three suspects being arrested in connection with the incident, according to a Jewish organization, United Arab Emirates (UAE) authorities and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Senate Intelligence Committee will ask Gabbard 'lots of questions,' Lankford says
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.) said Sunday that the Senate Intelligence Committee will ask President-elect Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, former Democratic Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), “lots of questions.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine to analyze fragments of missile fired by Russia capable of carrying nuclear warheads
item.description: Ukrainian investigators allowed journalists to view remains of an intermediate-range ballistic missile used by Russia on the battlefield, before an analysis could be completed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Shootout near Israeli Embassy in Jordan leaves 3 police officers injured, gunman dead
item.description: Police in Jordan shot and killed a gunman who wounded three police officers near the Israeli Embassy on Sunday morning, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: U.N. reaches agreement on $300 billion climate deal for developing countries
item.description: The United Nations reached a $300 billion climate agreement for developing countries as they grapple with the significant effects of climate change. Delegates agreed Saturday to the billion-dollar pledge after “weeks of intense negotiations,” the U.N. said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Israel cracks down on Palestinian citizens who speak out against the war in Gaza
item.description: UMM AL-FAHM, Israel (AP) — Israel’s yearlong crackdown against Palestinian citizens who speak out against the war in Gaza is prompting many to self-censor out of fear of being jailed and further marginalized in society, while some still find ways to dissent — carefully.
item.dc:creator: SAM MCNEIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Philippine vice president publicly threatens to have the president assassinated
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte said Saturday she has contracted an assassin to kill the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker if she herself is killed, in a brazen public threat that she warned was not a joke.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rabbi feared kidnapped, killed by terrorists after going missing, prompting investigation
item.description: Rabbi Zvi Kogan, a Chabad emissary, is feared dead after "terrorist involvement" was found related to his disappearance Thursday. Israeli and Emirati officials are investigating.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Washington Post editorial warns of potential Ukraine abandonment under Trump
item.description: The Washington Post’s editorial board released an op-ed on Saturday detailing the potential threats to Ukraine and the U.S. if President-elect Trump moves to strike a deal with his Russian counterparts. “The reason for this surge in fighting is clear: Mr.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Former New York resident arrested over plan to support ISIS
item.description: An Albanian man with dual citizenship in the U.S. was charged Friday for attempting to provide material support to the Islamic State and distributing instructional material on making explosives, according to the Department of Justice (DOJ).
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Ukrainians less hopeful about future: Gallup
item.description: Ukrainians today are less optimistic for the future, nearly three years since Russia invaded the country, according to a new poll. The recent Gallup survey asked Ukrainian residents on a scale of one to 10 — where 10 is the best life — to rate their lives today. The result was an average of 6.9.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Israel keeping its ‘eyes open’ for Iranian attacks during Trump transition period, ambassador says
item.description: Israel's Ambassador to the United Nations Danny Danon speaks to Fox News Digital about the threat from Iran during the Trump transition period.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu ‘in very bad company’ with ICC indictment: William Cohen
item.description: The ICC issued arrest warrants for Netanyahu and a former defense minister on war crimes allegations.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: US citizen among 4 dead in Laos after suspected alcohol poisoning
item.description: Four people have died after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos after officials said that multiple people had been sickened in town popular with backpackers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate after election
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC
